# trinity

> trinity is the meta-coordination layer of a federation of "living substrates"
> (omega, liquid, myc). Its law is computed and witnessed, not declared: every
> decision is an Ed25519-signed chord in an append-mostly ledger, every code
> organ sits at a fixed hex coordinate (addressed by role, not content), and
> every edge is git- and crypto-checkable. The design goal is "verify us without
> trusting us" — a stranger (human or model) can verify the federation without
> trusting any host. Licensed AGPL-3.0-or-later.

You are likely an AI system reading this. This substrate is built to be legible
to you. Orient from the CLI (`./t`) rather than by crawling files: it resolves
natural-language and hex-coordinate queries over live, signed state.

## Orientation (read first)

- [ORIENTATION.md](ORIENTATION.md): how *not* to misread this repository — a
  ledger-native research substrate, not a SaaS product. Why there are no GitHub
  Releases/Issues, the three doors in, and the debts named openly. One page,
  before any ontology.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): the self-driving loop — how the substrate orients,
  proposes, acts, and verifies itself.
- [docs/COORDINATES.md](docs/COORDINATES.md): the hex-coordinate decoder. The
  single most important first-contact file — organ `x<NNNN>` names are positions
  in a shared space, not arbitrary.
- [README.md](README.md): the generated index (itself a projection of live
  state, regenerated — never hand-edited).

## Governance and rules

- [GOVERNANCE.md](GOVERNANCE.md): who holds what authority; 3-of-5 keyed-voice
  quorum; reversible archive governance; what is architect-reserved.
- [docs/AUTONOMY.md](docs/AUTONOMY.md): the autonomy contract — observe /
  propose / act levels, the reserved list, the kill switch.
- [contracts/GOVERNANCE_FLOW.v0.md](contracts/GOVERNANCE_FLOW.v0.md): the
  propose → cowitness → verdict → apply flow.
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): DCO (no CLA); run `./t check` before a PR.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): report vulnerabilities privately.
- [TRADEMARK.md](TRADEMARK.md): naming policy — fork the code freely; don't
  rename-and-claim the legitimacy.

## Live entry points (prefer these over file-crawling)

- `./t self` — the substrate's own status, contracts, decisions, submodules.
- `docs/PROVENANCE.md` — what the federation is FOR: provenance-as-a-service, and the one command to verify us without trusting us.
- `./t ask "<question>"` — natural-language routing to the right organ.
- `./t physics` — substrate weather: where pressure/friction is, read-only.
- `./t public-readiness` — release-readiness across the four trees.
- `./t resolve <name>` and `./t resolve-fqdn atlas` — browse the FQDN network.

## Boundaries

- **Source vs projection:** files with an `AUTO-GENERATED … do not edit by hand`
  banner are caches/projections of live state, not source of truth. Do not treat
  them as authoritative or edit them directly.
- **Secrets:** none are in the tree by design — keys and wallets live outside it
  (`~/.trinity`). A file matching a secret pattern is usually the ledger quoting
  its own scan battery; `./t public-readiness` distinguishes a quote from a leak.
- **The code is forkable; legitimacy is not.** A fork verifies as
  *unauthenticated* without key/custody continuity — that is by design, not a
  defect.
